viral_posts.hook: Day 114: How to Not Be a Psychopath
viral_posts.caption:
Day 114: How to Not Be a Psychopath

You don’t trust happiness.

You trust struggle.

A lot of people don’t realize they’ve spent so much time surviving that peace starts to feel suspicious.

The reading says that good feelings can actually make us anxious.

Not because something is wrong.

Because something is right.

And if you’ve spent years carrying stress, chaos, heartbreak, or uncertainty, joy can feel unfamiliar.

So when life finally starts getting better, you start looking for what’s wrong with it.

Maybe happiness isn’t the thing leaving.

Maybe you’re just not letting it stay.

Today’s reading is from The Language of Letting Go by Melody Beattie.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you want to do hard things, you can do hard things. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together.

I love y’all.

Peace.

viral_posts.caption: If she wanted to, she would

viral_posts.hook: The people who had access to you had no idea how unreachable you are to other people until
viral_posts.caption: The people who had access to you had no idea how unreachable you are to other people until they became other people.

viral_posts.hook: This is a hard truth I’ve had to admit to myself:
viral_posts.caption:
This is a hard truth I’ve had to admit to myself:

I think every woman I’ve ever loved, I’ve loved more than she loved me.

For years, I thought I just had bad luck.

Now I think I had a pattern.

I was attracted to emotionally unavailable women because the chase felt familiar. Somewhere along the way, I learned that love was something you had to earn.

The crazy part?

When secure, healthy women came into my life, I wasn’t interested. It felt boring. It didn’t create the same spark.

But the spark wasn’t love.

It was anxiety.

I’m finally learning that chemistry and compatibility are not the same thing. And sometimes what feels like love is just an old wound looking for a familiar home.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you wanna do hard shit, you can do hard shit. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together. I love y’all. Peace.

viral_posts.caption: You are picking the person who will be with you on the worst day of your life. Do not take that lightly.

viral_posts.hook: The system I have used to grow to over 200k followers in about 6-7 months. Behind the curt
viral_posts.caption: The system I have used to grow to over 200k followers in about 6-7 months. Behind the curtain if you will ❤️

viral_posts.hook: I’ve spent a lot of my life believing the answer was more.
viral_posts.caption:
I’ve spent a lot of my life believing the answer was more.

More information.
More work.
More goals.
More productivity.

But lately I’ve been realizing that every time I feel overwhelmed, disconnected, or lost, the solution is almost never addition.

It’s subtraction.

A long walk without my phone.
A quiet morning.
A few hours without anyone else’s opinions in my head.

Blaise Pascal wrote that many of humanity’s problems come from our inability to sit quietly alone in a room.

The older I get, the more I think he’s right.

Because every time I get quiet enough, I hear it.

That still, small voice underneath all the noise.

The one that usually knows exactly what I need.

Sometimes growth isn’t about becoming more.

It’s about removing everything that isn’t you.

viral_posts.hook: Day 115 of How to Not Be a Psychopath
viral_posts.caption:
Day 115 of How to Not Be a Psychopath

You’re not confused.

You just don’t like the answer.

A lot of us spend years calling it confusion when it’s really fear.

Fear of disappointing people.
Fear of conflict.
Fear of being misunderstood.

The reading says that we can convince ourselves that people-pleasing, going against our nature, and not being honest is the kind thing to do.

But eventually that becomes exhausting.

Because deep down, most of the time, you already know what you want.

You know what needs to be said.
You know what needs to end.
You know what needs to begin.

The real question isn’t, “What should I do?”

The real question is, “Am I willing to be honest with myself?”

Today’s reading is from The Language of Letting Go by Melody Beattie.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you want to do hard things, you can do hard things. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together. I love y’all. Peace.
